"Besides controlling music and calls, you can operate the following additional functions on your earphones:1.  **Activate Phone Voice Assistant**    Press and hold the Multifunction Button until you hear the first beep, then let go.    <PIC>2.  **Activate Music App**    Press and hold until the first beep, then let go. The music app will resume playback from where you left off. If there was no previous session, tap the button again and the app will play songs it thinks you'll like.    <PIC>3.  **Turn Ambient Awareness and ANC Modes ON/OFF**    Press the left earbud to cycle through the modes.    <PIC>4.  **Turn Low Latency Mode ON/OFF**    Press and hold the button until you hear the second beep, then let go. This mode reduces audio delay and is best for lip-sync and gaming.    <PIC>5.  **Use Multipoint Bluetooth Connection**    This allows you to connect to two devices simultaneously (e.g., a smartphone and a computer):    *   Go to the Bluetooth menu on the device already paired to your earbuds and turn Bluetooth OFF. The earbuds will disconnect and become available for pairing.    *   Pair the earbuds to your second device.    *   Turn Bluetooth back ON on your first device. The earbuds will auto-reconnect to it. You can now use either device for music or calls.You can also customize button actions, enable hands-free voice control, and update firmware through the earphones App.", ["earphones_01", "earphones_02", "earphones_03", "Manual12_10"]

"If your earphones are unresponsive or not working, there are two types of resets you can perform: a factory reset or a hardware reset. The earbuds must be disconnected from Bluetooth and removed from the charging case before you start.**1. Factory Reset**This will erase all of your settings and restore the earbuds to their original out-of-the-box state.*   Press and hold the button for **6 seconds**.<PIC>**2. Hardware Reset**You should only use this in the rare case that the earbuds have locked up and are completely unresponsive.*   Press and hold the button for **10 seconds**. The earbuds will restart.*   After the reset, press the button again to power them back on.<PIC>*   During a hardware reset, the lights will flash red and blue.", ["Manual12_11", "Manual12_12"]

Here are the maintenance and care policies for your earphones:1.  **Cleaning:** Use a soft cloth or paper towel to clean your earphones. Never use any harsh chemicals or detergents for cleaning. Make sure your earphones are dry before charging.2.  **Storage:** When your earphones are not in use, they should be stored in a cool, dry place.3.  **Cable Handling:** Never tug or yank on a cable while it is connected to your charging case. Connect and disconnect cables as carefully as possible.4.  **Temperature Exposure:** Never expose your earphones to high temperatures or extreme cold.5.  **Disposal:** Please recycle or dispose of your earphones properly based on the laws and rules of your municipality. Contact local recycling facilities and/or the manufacturer of your earphones for further information.

"Based on the provided manual, here are the buttons and interfaces of this eReader from three different views:**FRONT VIEW**<PIC>*   **[1] Home/ESC button:** Cancels operations or returns to the previous interface.*   **[2] Prev/Next Page:** Turns to the previous or next page.*   **[3] Navigation/Menu:** A 4-way control (left, right, up, down) for navigation, and confirm.*   **[4] Zoom in/out:** Zooms in/out of JPEG files or changes E-book font size.*   **[5] Rotate:** Rotates the E-book view horizontally or vertically.*   **[6] 3.5 mm Earphone Jack:** For connecting earphones.*   **[7] USB Port:** For charging and connecting to a computer.*   **[8] Micro SD card reader:** Compatible with Micro SD/SDHC up to 16GB.*   **[9] Play/Pause:** Starts or pauses music or MIC recording playback.*   **[10] Power button:** Press for 3-5 seconds to power on/off.*   **[11] Volume +:** Increases the volume.*   **[12] Volume -:** Reduces the volume.*   **[14] Speaker:** 0.5W audio output.*   **[15] TFT LCD Display Screen:** 7” diagonal.**NAVIGATION BUTTON VIEW**<PIC>*   Navigation button: Use the directional keys to select up, down, left, or right.*   Confirm/Enter: Press the "M" key to enter the selected function.*   ESC: Press the "Home/ESC" button to return to the previous screen.**BOTTOM VIEW**<PIC>*   **[13] Reset On/Off switch:** Located on the back of the device next to the card reader, used to reset the device to factory defaults.", ["Manual13_0", "Manual13_1", "Manual13_2"]

"The Main Menu is the starting interface that lists all the available features on your eReader. From here, you can select and enter functions like "Browser History," "eBook," "Music," "Video," "Photo," "Record," "Explorer," and "Settings."  <PIC>The Browser History function displays a list of recently browsed files. You can use the Navigation keys to select a book and press the "M" button to open it directly to the last page you were reading.  <PIC>  Please note, if the files you recently read were stored on a Micro SD card and have since been deleted, the records will still remain in the "Browser History" list, but you will not be able to open them.", ["Manual13_3", "Manual13_4"]

"When in eBook mode, pressing the "M" button will bring up a menu with the following options: "Page Jump", "Save Mark", "Load Mark", "Del Mark", "Browser Mode", "Flip Time", "Brightness", and "Set color". <PIC>", ["eReader_08"]

"Here’s what to do to listen to music on your eReader:1.  From the Main Menu, select **“Music”** and press the **“M”** button to enter.2.  Choose the audio file you want to play and press **“M”** to start playback.You can add your own songs by connecting the device to your computer with a USB cable and copying your audio files (MP3, WMA, WAV, ACC, etc.) into it. A nice feature is that you can listen to music while reading an eBook or browsing photos.**Controls while music is playing:**-   **Volume:** Press the **Up/Down** navigation keys.-   **Play/Pause:** Press the **Play/Pause** button.-   **Skip/Seek:** Press and release the **left/right** direction keys to go to the previous or next song. "To play a video on your eReader, go to the main menu and select "Video," then press the "M" key to enter the Video mode. <PIC> This device supports AVI, RMVB, and MPEG2 video formats.", ["Manual13_9"]

"**Yes, it can function as a photo viewer.**  It supports JPEG and BMP image formats and can be operated directly from the main menu.**How to use the Photo feature:**1.  **Enter Photo Mode**      From the main menu, select "Photo" and press the **"M" button** to enter. <PIC>2.  **Browse Photos**      Press the **left/right navigation keys** to go to the previous or next photo.3.  **Rotate a Photo**      Press the **"Play/Pause" key** to rotate the current photo in a counter-clockwise sequence.      *Note: Larger files may take more time to rotate.*4.  **Zoom In/Out**      Press the dedicated zoom keys to zoom in or out of the photo.      *Note: Large files also require more time to load into the photo viewer.* <PIC>", ["Manual13_3", "Manual13_10"]

Based on the user manual, you might encounter the following issues and their solutions:1.  **Cannot Power On**    *   Charge the device until the power indicator bar is full.    *   If it still doesn't work, the device may be defective; contact the manufacturer.2.  **Not Able to Charge**    *   The AC adaptor may be defective; contact the manufacturer for a replacement.    *   The USB Cable may be defective; contact the manufacturer for a replacement.3.  **No Sound or Noise When Using Earphone**    *   Adjust the volume setting to make sure it is not at the 0 position.    *   Try another pair of earphones.    *   Ensure the music file compression rate is high, as low-quality music files may cause noise and distortion.4.  **Cannot Download Files into the Reader**    *   The USB cable may be defective.    *   The e-reader's memory is full.    *   Windows 98 may require a new USB 2.0 driver to detect the e-book reader as a removable disk (please visit a website to download the driver).5.  **Cannot Play Music Files**    *   The reader is able to play most popular audio formats, but some latest music formats or resolutions might not work properly. Contact manufacturer tech support for assistance.

When using this fax, pay attention to the following safety precautions to reduce the risk of fire, electrical shock, and injury:1.  **Avoid Water and Moisture:** Do not use the product near water or locations that may become wet, such as near a bathtub, wash bowl, kitchen sink, washing machine, in a wet basement, or near a swimming pool.2.  **Avoid Thunderstorms:** Avoid using the product during a thunderstorm, as there is a remote risk of electrical shock from lightning.3.  **Do Not Report Gas Leaks Nearby:** Do not use this product to report a gas leak if you are in the vicinity of the leak.4.  **Use the Provided Power Cord:** Use only the power cord provided with the product.5.  **Unplug if Damaged or Malfunctioning:** Immediately unplug the product from the AC power outlet if it becomes unusually hot, releases smoke, generates strong smells, or if liquid is spilled on it. Also unplug it and refer to service personnel if the product has been exposed to rain or water, does not operate normally, or exhibits a distinct change in performance.6.  **Do Not Service Yourself:** Do not attempt to service this product yourself, as opening or removing covers may expose you to dangerous voltage and void your warranty.7.  **Telephone Line Caution:** Never touch uninsulated telephone wires or terminals unless the telephone line has been unplugged from the wall jack. Never install telephone wiring during a thunderstorm or in a wet location.

"When using the fax, to keep your fingers safe, pay attention to the following points:1.  **Wait for pages to fully exit** the product before picking them up. Failure to do so may trap your fingers inside the machine and cause injury.2.  **Do not put your hand or any foreign objects into the ink insertion slot**, as this may cause injury.3.  **Be careful when the paper tray is extended** to hold large paper. Position the product so the tray does not protrude past the edge of the table or desk; if someone hits the tray, the product could fall and cause injury.4.  **Avoid putting your fingers in the specific areas shown in the illustrations** to prevent injuries.    <PIC>    <PIC>5.  **When changing the tray size** from large back to the original size, be careful not to pinch your fingers in the gaps or slots in the bottom of the tray.    <PIC>6.  **For specific models (MFC-J6540DW/MFC-J6555DW/MFC-J6740DW/MFC-J6940DW/MFC-J6955DW)**, be careful not to put your fingers in the areas shown in the illustrations.    <PIC>7.  **Do not run your hand over the edge of the paper**, as the thin and sharp edges may cause an injury.", ["Manual15_6", "Manual15_7", "Manual15_8", "Manual15_9"]

"Moving a fax machine safely involves several important precautions to prevent injury and damage. Here is what you need to pay attention to:1.  **Get Help for Heavy Models**: If you have a larger model (MFC-J5955DW/MFC-J6540DW/MFC-J6555DW/MFC-J6740DW/MFC-J6940DW/MFC-J6955DW), at least two people should lift it. One person should hold the front and the other the back.    <PIC>2.  **Use the Correct Handholds**: Always carry the product by sliding your hands into the handhold indentations located on each side of the product. For the larger models mentioned above, do **not** carry it by holding the areas shown in the illustrations, as it may slip and cause injury.    <PIC>    <PIC>3.  **Remove Expanded Paper Trays**: If you have extended the trays to hold large paper, you must remove them before carrying the product. The weight of the paper could cause the trays to fall and cause injury.    <PIC>4.  **Secure the Paper Tray Position**: If the tray has been extended for large paper and you are not removing it, position the product so the tray does not protrude past the edge of the table or desk. Someone could hit the tray, causing the product to fall.5.  **Watch Your Fingers**: Be careful not to put your fingers in the areas shown in the illustrations to prevent pinching or trapping them. This is especially important when setting the product down.    <PIC>    <PIC>6.  **Secure Cables**: Ensure that all cables and cords leading to the product are secured so they do not pose a tripping hazard during the move.As a final check, confirm that the new location is a flat, level, stable surface, away from busy areas where the product could be knocked over.", ["fax_08", "Manual15_10", "Manual15_11", "Manual15_12", "Manual15_7", "Manual15_8"]

"Here are the steps to connect the regulator to the LP tank:1.  Make sure the LP tank is properly secured onto the grill.2.  Turn all control knobs to the OFF position.3.  Turn the LP tank OFF by turning the OPD hand wheel clockwise to a full stop.4.  Remove the protective cap from the LP tank valve. Always use the cap and strap supplied with the valve.    <PIC>    Do not use a POL transport plug (plastic part with external threads). It will defeat the safety feature of the valve.    <PIC>5.  Hold the regulator and insert the nipple into the LP tank valve. Hand-tighten the coupling nut, holding the regulator in a straight line with the LP tank valve so as not to cross-thread. The nipple has to be centered into the LP tank valve.    <PIC>6.  Turn the coupling nut clockwise and tighten to a full stop. The regulator will seal on the back-check feature in the LP tank valve, resulting in some resistance. An additional one-half to three-quarters turn is required to complete the connection. Tighten by hand only — do not use tools.", ["Manual19_16", "Manual19_17", "Manual19_18"]

"To ensure your safety when using the grill, follow the steps below to perform leak testing on the valves, hose, and regulator.**1. Prepare for the Test***   Turn all grill control knobs to the OFF position.*   Ensure the regulator is tightly connected to the LP tank.*   Completely open the LP tank valve by turning the OPD hand wheel counterclockwise. If you hear a rushing sound, turn the gas off immediately. There is a major leak at the connection. Correct this before proceeding by calling Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.**2. Apply Soapy Solution***   Brush a soapy solution onto the areas indicated in the picture below.<PIC>**3. Check for Leaks***   If “growing” bubbles appear, there is a leak. Close the LP tank valve immediately and retighten connections. If leaks cannot be stopped, do not try to repair it. Call Sears for replacement parts at 1-800-4-MY-HOME.*   Always close the LP tank valve after performing the leak test by turning the hand wheel clockwise.<PIC>**4. Perform Hose and Valve Checks***   **Hose Check:** Before each use, check to see if hoses are cut, worn, or kinked. Replace damaged hoses before using the grill. Use only the valve/hose/regulator specified in the Parts List.<PIC>*   **Valve Check:** Make sure the gas is off at the LP tank before checking valves. Knobs lock in the OFF position. To check valves, first push in knobs and release; they should spring back. If knobs do not spring back, replace the valve assembly before using the grill. Turn knobs to the LO position and then back to OFF. They should turn smoothly.**Important Safety Reminders***   Do not use the grill until it is leak-tested.<PIC>*   If you cannot complete the connection, disconnect the regulator and repeat steps 5 and 6 of the connection process. If you are still unable to complete the connection, do not use this regulator!<PIC>*   If a leak is detected at any time, STOP and call the fire department.*   If you cannot stop a gas leak, immediately close the LP tank valve and call your LP gas supplier or fire department.", ["Manual19_20", "Manual19_21", "Manual19_30", "Manual19_22", "Manual19_19"]

"Based on the instructions, here's what you should pay attention to for indirect cooking:1.  **Always cook with the lid closed.** This is essential for the method to work correctly.    <PIC>2.  **Adjust for weather conditions.** Cooking times may vary, and during cold and windy conditions, you may need to increase the temperature setting to ensure sufficient cooking temperatures.3.  **Use a low temperature setting.** This produces slow, even heating and is ideal for slow roasting and baking.4.  **Understand how it works.** The heat from the selected burners circulates gently throughout the grill, cooking the food without the touch of a direct flame. This method greatly reduces flare-ups when cooking extra fatty cuts.    <PIC>5.  **Use the 1-burner cooking method for smaller meals.** This method, which can be used with direct or indirect heat, is best for smaller meals or foods and consumes less fuel.", ["Manual19_37", "Manual19_36"]

"根据手册，组装过程的前三步如下：1.  **将灯适配器安装到后面板上**：使用四颗螺丝将其固定。2.  **两人协作将烤炉头部抬起并放置到推车上**：小心地将烤炉头部放下，对齐烤炉头部底部的槽口与推车侧板上的立柱，确保调节器软管悬挂在推车内部。烤炉头部必须面向推车的开口侧。    <PIC>3.  **安装侧燃烧器阀支架**：首先拆下出厂时固定在支架上的两颗螺丝和锁紧垫圈，将支架放置在侧燃烧器搁板下方，使阀杆从面板中心孔伸出。对齐支架与面板上的左右孔，用刚拆下的锁紧垫圈和螺丝固定。接着，将侧燃烧器面板放在阀杆上，对齐小孔，确保“OFF”字样位于顶部，并用两颗螺丝固定。", ["Manual19_53"]

"Of course! Here are some important safety tips to follow when using your grill:*   Before opening the LP tank valve, check the coupling nut for tightness.*   When the grill is not in use, turn off all control knobs and the LP tank valve.*   Never move the grill while it is in operation or still hot.*   Use long-handled barbecue utensils and oven mitts to avoid burns and splatters.*   The maximum load for shelves is 10 lbs. Do not use a cooking pot larger than 9" on the grid.*   The grease tray must be installed into the opening in the back panel and should be emptied after each use. Do not remove the grease tray until the grill has completely cooled.*   If you notice grease or other hot material dripping from the grill onto the valve, hose, or regulator, turn off the gas supply at once. Determine the cause, correct it, then clean and inspect the valve, hose, and regulator before continuing. Perform a leak test.*   The regulator may make a humming or whistling noise during operation. This will not affect the safety or use of the grill.*   If the regulator frosts, turn off the grill and LP tank valve immediately. This indicates a problem with the tank and it should not be used on any product. Return it to the supplier!<PIC>*   If the burner does not light, turn the knobs to OFF, wait 5 minutes, and try again. Always close the valve during the 5-minute waiting period. If the burner does not ignite with the valve open, gas will continue to flow out of the burner and could accidentally ignite with risk of injury.Here is a visual summary of these safety tips:<PIC>Lastly, before each use, check to see if hoses are cut, worn, or kinked. Replace damaged hoses before using the grill.<PIC>", ["Manual19_25", "Manual19_24", "Manual19_30"]

"Identification numbers are unique codes stamped on labels or plates attached to specific parts of your watercraft. They are essential for ordering parts or recovering the jetski if it is stolen. Here are the main ones:1.  **Primary Identification (PRI-ID) Number**    This number is on a label attached inside the engine compartment.    <PIC>    <PIC>2.  **Hull Identification Number (HIN)**    This number is stamped on a plate attached to the hull on the aft, starboard (right) side.    <PIC>    <PIC>3.  **Engine Serial Number**    This number is on a label attached to the engine unit itself.    <PIC>    <PIC>It is recommended that you record all these numbers and keep them in a separate, safe location away from the watercraft.", ["Manual20_0", "jetski_01", "Manual20_1", "jetski_02", "Manual20_2", "jetski_03"]

"Here are the cruising limitations you should follow:*   Scan constantly for people, objects, and other watercraft. Be alert for conditions that limit your visibility or block your vision of others.    <PIC>*   Operate defensively at safe speeds and keep a safe distance away from people, objects, and other watercraft.*   Do not follow directly behind watercraft or other boats. Do not go near others to spray or splash them with water.*   Avoid sharp turns or other maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you or understand where you are going.*   Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water.*   Ride within your limits and avoid aggressive maneuvers to reduce the risk of loss of control, ejection, and collision.*   Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather or when visibility is poor; this may lead to an accident causing injury or death. Be alert to the possibility of adverse weather. Take note of weather forecasts and the prevailing weather conditions before setting out.*   As with any water sport, you should not ride your watercraft without someone else near by. If you ride further than swimming distance from shore, you should be accompanied by another boat or watercraft, but make sure you stay a safe distance away.    <PIC>*   Never operate in water that is less than 60 cm (2 ft) deep, otherwise you increase your chance of hitting a submerged object, which could result in injury.*   This is a high performance boat—not a toy. Sharp turns or jumping wakes or waves can increase the risk of back/spinal injury (paralysis), facial injuries, and broken legs, ankles and other bones. Do not jump wakes or waves.    <PIC>*   This watercraft is not equipped with lighting required for night operation. Do not operate the watercraft after sunset or before dawn, otherwise you increase the risk of colliding with another boat, which could result in severe injury or death.", ["Manual20_16", "Manual20_17", "Manual20_18"]

"Here are the cruising limitations for your watercraft:- **Constant Scanning:** Scan constantly for people, objects, and other watercraft. Be alert for conditions that limit your visibility or block your vision of others.  <PIC>- **Defensive Operation:** Operate defensively at safe speeds and keep a safe distance away from people, objects, and other watercraft.  - **Following & Splashing:** Do not follow directly behind watercraft or other boats. Do not go near others to spray or splash them with water.  - **Maneuvers:** Avoid sharp turns or other maneuvers that make it hard for others to avoid you or understand where you are going.  <PIC>- **Submerged Objects & Shallow Water:** Avoid areas with submerged objects or shallow water. Never operate in water that is less than 60 cm (2 ft) deep, otherwise you increase your chance of hitting a submerged object, which could result in injury.  - **Rough Conditions:** Do not operate the watercraft in rough water, bad weather, or when visibility is poor; this may lead to an accident causing injury or death.  - **Night Operation:** This watercraft is not equipped with lighting required for night operation. Do not operate the watercraft after sunset or before dawn, otherwise you increase the risk of colliding with another boat, which could result in severe injury or death.  <PIC>- **Jumping Wakes:** This is a high-performance boat—not a toy. Do not jump wakes or waves. Sharp turns or jumping wakes or waves can increase the risk of back/spinal injury (paralysis), facial injuries, and broken legs, ankles, and other bones.  - **Ride Within Limits:** Ride within your limits and avoid aggressive maneuvers to reduce the risk of loss of control, ejection, and collision.  - **Buddy System:** As with any water sport, you should not ride your watercraft without someone else nearby. If you ride further than swimming distance from shore, you should ride by another boat or watercraft, but make sure you stay a safe distance away.", ["Manual20_16", "Manual20_17", "Manual20_18"]

"Before you operate the watercraft, you must complete the following:1.  **Read the provided materials** – Read the Owner’s/Operator’s Manual, the Riding Practice Guide, the Riding Instruction card, and all warning and caution labels on the watercraft.2.  **Watch the video** – Watch the Basic Orientation Video provided with your watercraft.3.  **Secure the storage compartments** – Make sure that the storage compartments are properly secured before operating. Note that the compartments are not waterproof; if you carry items that must stay dry (like the manuals), put them in a waterproof bag.4.  **Carry the manual and tool kit** – It is advisable to always carry the Owner’s/Operator’s Manual and tool kit with you. A storage compartment is provided for this.    <PIC>5.  **Never allow untrained operators** – Never allow anyone to operate the watercraft until they have also read all the materials, warning labels, and if possible, watched the Basic Orientation Video.", ["Manual20_80"]

"Your jet ski is water-jet propelled, with the jet pump directly connected to the engine. This means jet thrust will produce some movement whenever the engine is running—there is no “neutral” position.  Jet thrust turns the watercraft. Releasing the throttle completely produces only minimum thrust. If you are traveling at speeds above trolling, you will have rapidly decreasing ability to steer without throttle. You may still have some turning ability immediately after releasing the throttle, but once the engine slows down, the watercraft will no longer respond to handlebar input until you open the throttle again or you reach a trolling speed.  <PIC>  For safety, keep away from the intake grate ① while the engine is on. Items such as long hair, loose clothing, or PFD straps can become entangled in moving parts, resulting in severe injury or drowning. Never insert any object into the jet thrust nozzle ② while the engine is running—severe injury or death could result from coming in contact with the rotating parts of the jet pump.  <PIC>  Before removing any debris or weeds that may have collected around the jet intake, stop the engine and remove the clip ③ from the engine shut-off switch ④. Practice turning in an open area without obstructions until you have a good feel for this maneuver.", ["Manual20_24", "Manual20_25"]

"When operating your watercraft (jetski), you must follow the **Steering and Sailing Rules**. These rules determine which vessel is the "stand-on" vessel (has the right-of-way) and which is the "give-way" vessel (must yield).The rules cover three main encounter situations:*   **Meeting:** Approaching another vessel head-on.*   **Crossing:** Traveling across another vessel's path.*   **Overtaking:** Passing or being passed by another vessel.In the following illustration, your watercraft is in the center. You should give the right-of-way to any vessels shown in the white area (you are the give-way vessel). Any vessels in the shaded area must yield to you (they are the give-way vessels). Both you and the meeting vessel must alter course to avoid each other.<PIC>As the **give-way vessel**, you have a duty to take positive and timely action to stay out of the way. Normally, you should not cross in front of the vessel with the right-of-way. Slow down or change directions briefly and pass behind the other vessel. Always move in a way that the other operator can see what you are doing.Also, always remember the **General Prudential Rule**: if a collision appears unavoidable, neither boat has the right-of-way. Both vessels must act immediately to avoid the collision.", ["Manual20_26"]

"To remove and install the seat of your jetski, please follow these steps:**To remove the seat:**1.  There is a seat latch at the rear of the seat. Pull this latch up.2.  Pull the seat off.<PIC>**To install the seat:**1.  Insert the projection on the front of the seat into the stay on the deck.2.  Push the rear of the seat down to lock it in place securely.<PIC>**Important Cautions:***   Do not route ropes or tie-downs over the seat, as they may leave permanent marks on the seat’s surface.*   When securing the watercraft, wrap any ropes or tie-downs with towels or rags where they touch the body of the watercraft to avoid scratching or damage.<PIC>", ["Manual20_36", "Manual20_37", "Manual20_78"]

"To open the hood, push the latch ① down, and then lift the hood up.  <PIC>  To close the hood, push the hood down to lock it in place.  <PIC>  **NOTE:** Make sure that the hood is properly secured before operating the watercraft.", ["Manual20_38", "Manual20_39"]

"The two filler caps you’ve noticed are the **fuel tank filler cap** and the **oil tank filler cap**. Both are removed in the same way:1.  **Turn the cap counterclockwise.**    *   **Fuel tank filler cap:** Turn cap ① counterclockwise to remove.        <PIC>    *   **Oil tank filler cap:** Turn cap ① counterclockwise to remove.        <PIC>**Important Reminder:**Before operating the watercraft, ensure both caps are properly secured by tightening them clockwise.", ["Manual20_40", "Manual20_41"]

"To start the engine, push the start switch (green button) to start the engine.  <PIC>To stop the engine normally, push the engine stop switch (red button).  <PIC>**Important Notes:***   The engine will not start if the clip is removed from the engine shut-off switch.*   Do not run the engine at full throttle for more than 15 seconds to check the meter for operation on land, as the engine could overheat.", ["Manual20_48", "Manual20_43"]

"There are two levers on the jetski: the throttle lever and the choke lever. Here’s how to use each one:### 1. Throttle Lever- **To accelerate:** Squeeze the throttle lever to increase engine speed.- **To decelerate or idle:** Release the throttle lever to decrease engine speed or return it to the idle position.<PIC>### 2. Choke Lever- **To turn the choke on (for starting a cold engine):** Move the lever in direction $\circledast$.- **To turn the choke off:** Move the lever in direction $\circledcirc$.<PIC>The choke supplies a richer air-fuel mixture necessary for starting a cold engine.", ["Manual20_47", "Manual20_46"]

"The QSTS selector is located at the left handlebar grip and is used to adjust the trim angle of the watercraft by changing the angle of the jet thrust nozzle vertically. There are 5 positions: 2 bow down, neutral, and 2 bow up.To change the trim angle:1. Reduce engine speed to 3,000 r/min or less.2. Squeeze the shift lock lever, and then turn the QSTS selector to the desired position.3. Release the shift lock lever to lock the QSTS selector.<PIC>Important cautions:*   Do not turn the QSTS selector while operating the watercraft at full throttle, otherwise damage could occur to the QSTS.*   The neutral "N" position will provide good performance for most operating conditions. To enhance particular types of performance, select bow down or bow up.<PIC>", ["Manual20_51", "Manual20_52"]

"The fuel meter and hour meter on your jetski provide the following functions:**Fuel Meter**The fuel meter is provided for convenient fuel level checking while riding. It has eight segments which show the amount of fuel remaining in the fuel tank. The indication of the segments differs based on your operating condition, so use the meter as a reference.<PIC>**Hour Meter**The hour meter is provided to make it easy to follow the maintenance schedule. It shows the hours of engine operation that have elapsed since the watercraft was new. The elapsed time will be kept even if the battery terminals have been disconnected.<PIC>If the hour meter malfunctions, a "_ - _ " display and the warning light will blink.", ["Manual20_58", "Manual20_59"]

"Inspecting the fuel filter and fuel tank is important, but these components require professional servicing and should not be handled by the owner.Here is how to approach the inspection:**Fuel Filter**This watercraft is equipped with a one-piece, disposable fuel filter. It should be replaced after the initial 10 hours or first month of operation and every 200 hours or 24 months thereafter, or if water is found in the filter.<PIC>**WARNING**Do not try to replace the fuel filter yourself. An incorrectly installed filter can leak gasoline, which could lead to fire or explosion. If necessary, have a Yamaha dealer replace the fuel filter.**Fuel Tank**You should check the fuel tank for leakage or water in the tank. If water is found in the fuel system or if the fuel tank needs to be cleaned, have a Yamaha dealer service the watercraft.<PIC>For your safety, any replacement or cleaning of these components should always be performed by a Yamaha dealer.", ["Manual20_81", "Manual20_82"]

"Here’s how to adjust the adjustable sponsons:1.  Remove the bolts ① on both sponsons.      <PIC>2.  Remove both sponsons, then install them in the desired position.      **NOTE:** Be sure to install both sponsons to the same level.3.  Tighten the bolts on both sponsons to the correct torque.      <PIC>      **Tightening torque:** 18 N·m (1.8 kgf·m, 13 ft·lb)For an explanation of how the various positions affect watercraft performance (such as straight-line or turning behavior), refer to the Adjustable Sponson section.", ["Manual20_83", "Manual20_84"]

"If the jet intake or impeller is clogged with weeds or debris, you need to beach the watercraft and clean them immediately. Continuing to operate in this condition can cause the engine to overheat and seize.**Before you begin, safety is critical:***   Stop the engine and remove the clip from the engine shut-off switch. Severe injury or death could result from contact with the rotating parts of the jet pump.**Cleaning Procedure:**1.  **Position the watercraft:** Turn the watercraft onto its port (left) side as shown. Place a suitable clean cloth or carpeting underneath to protect it from abrasions and scratches. Support the bow so the handlebars cannot be bent or damaged.    <PIC>2.  **Remove the debris:** Remove any weeds or debris from around the drive shaft, impeller, pump housing, and jet thrust nozzle.    If the debris is difficult to remove, consult your Yamaha dealer.", ["Manual20_86"]

"Here’s how to start your jetski in different situations, based on the manual.### 1. Standard starting procedure (when the watercraft is in the water)1.  **Attach the engine shut-off cord (lanyard) to your wrist or PFD.**2.  **Install the clip onto the engine shut-off switch.** The engine will not start unless the clip is in place.    <PIC>3.  **Press the start button.** The engine should turn over and start.### 2. Starting after removing debris or weedsIf you’ve stopped the engine to clear the jet intake, follow these steps carefully before restarting:1.  **Ensure the engine is stopped.**2.  **Remove the clip from the engine shut-off switch.** This is a critical safety step to prevent accidental starting while you are near the intake.    <PIC>3.  **Clear any debris or weeds from around the jet intake.**4.  **Re-install the clip onto the engine shut-off switch.**5.  **Press the start button.**### 3. Starting with a passengerBefore starting the watercraft with a passenger, ensure you meet these conditions:*   **Gain experience first.** You should have considerable practice and experience riding alone before attempting to carry a passenger.*   **Check the load limit.** The maximum load is **160 kg (353 lb)**. This total includes the weight of the operator, the passenger, and any cargo.*   **Do not exceed 2 persons.** The watercraft is designed for a maximum of **one operator and one passenger**.*   **Be aware of handling.** Operating with a passenger requires more skill. Become accustomed to the watercraft’s handling characteristics solo before trying any difficult maneuvers with a passenger on board.### Important starting safety reminders*   **There is no neutral.** The jet pump is directly connected to the engine, so jet thrust will produce some movement whenever the engine is running.*   **Keep clear of the intake grate and jet thrust nozzle.** Never insert any object into the jet thrust nozzle while the engine is running. Keep long hair, loose clothing, and PFD straps away from the intake grate to prevent entanglement and severe injury.    <PIC>", ["Manual20_24", "Manual20_24", "Manual20_25"]

"Here are the basic steps to board with a passenger in deep water:1.  **Board the watercraft yourself.** Climb on board as described for boarding alone, and sit astride the seat. Attach the engine shut-off cord to your wrist, then install the clip to the engine shut-off switch.2.  **Have the passenger approach.** Have the passenger move to the rear of the watercraft.3.  **Passenger boards.** Have the passenger get on board and sit on the seat.    <PIC>4.  **Check passenger positioning.** As the operator, check to see that the passenger has his or her feet on the floor of the footwell and is securely holding on to you.5.  **Start and go.** Start the engine, look in all directions, and then accelerate to planing speed.    <PIC>**Important balancing note:** When the passenger is getting on board, both of you should try to balance the watercraft.", ["Manual20_90", "Manual20_91"]

"If you’re having trouble balancing the jetski at a standstill, follow these steps:1. Let the passenger steady the watercraft while you board. Pull yourself up onto the platform into a seated position, then balance the watercraft.  <PIC>2. Attach the lanyard to your left wrist, then clip it onto the engine shut‑off switch. Start the engine and keep it at idle.  3. Have the passenger pull themselves up onto the platform, kneel down, and crawl onto the seat as the watercraft begins to accelerate.  <PIC>4. Once the passenger is on, accelerate to planing speed, then reduce the throttle to your desired running speed.  <PIC>Keep in mind that the heavier the total weight of the operator and passengers, the harder it will be to balance. Never operate the watercraft when the total weight (including any cargo) exceeds 160 kg (353 lb).", ["jetski_06", "jetski_07", "jetski_08"]
"The maximum load limit for the watercraft is **160 kg (353 lb)**. This is the total combined weight of the operator, any passenger, and all cargo.  <PIC>", ["Manual20_15"]

"To lower the roll bar, follow these steps. **Important:** Lower the roll bar only when absolutely necessary, as there is no rollover protection when it is down.1.  Remove the hairpin cotters and remove the 2 pins.    <PIC>2.  Lower the roll bar to the down position. Note that there are 2 down positions.3.  Install the 2 pins and secure them with the hairpin cotters.**Safety reminders when the roll bar is down:***   Do not wear the seat belt.*   Drive slowly and carefully.*   Raise the roll bar as soon as clearance permits.", ["Manual23_32"]

"To adjust the rear-shock assemblies on a lawn mower with a suspension system, follow these steps:1.  **Locate the rear-shock assemblies.** The slots for these assemblies have detent positions for reference, but you can position the assemblies anywhere in the slot, not just at the detents.2.  **Choose your desired firmness.** The following graphic shows the position for a soft or firm ride and the different detent positions:    *   Position 1 is the firmest setting.    *   Position 2 is the softest setting.    *   Position 3 indicates the detents in the slots.    <PIC>3.  **Adjust the assemblies.** Turn the seat-suspension knob to adjust the rear-shock assemblies.    <PIC>    *   **Important:** Ensure that the left and right rear-shock assemblies are always adjusted to the same positions.", ["Manual23_37", "Manual23_38"]

"For a lawn mower with an electric deck lift, you adjust the height of cut by raising or lowering the deck with a switch and then setting a pin in the desired position. Here are the steps:1.  **Raise or lower the deck:** Push up on the deck-lift switch to raise the deck, or push down on the switch to lower it ( Figure 31 ).      <PIC>2.  **Set the cutting height:** Select a hole in the height-of-cut bracket that corresponds to your desired cutting height, and insert the pin ( Figure 32 ).      <PIC>", ["Manual23_55", "Manual23_56"]
根据您提供的参考手册内容，其中并未包含关于如何装载（load）割草机的操作说明。手册涉及的内容包括避免割草过低、清洁与存放、割草盘下方清洁、停机以及割草盘维护等，但没有与装载运输相关的步骤。如果您需要装载割草机进行运输，通常需要遵循设备制造商提供的专用装载指南。建议您查阅该设备的操作员手册中关于“运输”或“装载”的章节，或联系授权经销商获取安全操作指导。

The provided manual does not contain instructions on how to unload a lawn mower. The manual covers cleaning, storage, cutting adjustments, stopping, and removing the mower deck, but it does not include a procedure for unloading the machine from a trailer or truck.

"Here’s how to change the engine oil of a lawn mower:1.  Start the engine and let it run for 5 minutes. This warms the oil so that it drains better.2.  Park the machine so that the drain side is slightly lower than the opposite side to ensure that the oil drains completely.3.  Disengage the blade-control switch (PTO) and engage the parking brake.4.  Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position.5.  Drain the oil from the engine (Figure 49).      <PIC>6.  Slowly pour approximately 80% of the specified oil into the filler tube and slowly add the additional oil to bring it to the Full mark (Figure 50).      <PIC>7.  Start the engine and drive to a flat area.8.  Check the oil level again.Dispose of the used oil at a recycling center.", ["Manual23_75", "Manual23_76"]

"To remove the filters from the lawn mower, follow these steps:1.  **Prepare the machine:** Park the mower on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake.2.  **Shut off the engine:** Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position.3.  **Open the air cleaner:** Release the latches on the air cleaner and pull the air-cleaner cover off the air-cleaner body.    <PIC>4.  **Remove the primary filter:** Gently slide the primary filter out of the air-cleaner body. Avoid knocking the filter into the side of the body.5.  **Remove the safety filter:** Remove the safety filter only if you are replacing it. Do not attempt to clean it; if it is dirty, the primary filter is damaged and both must be replaced.", ["Manual23_72"]

"To replace the mower belt, follow these steps:1.  **Prepare the machine:** Park the machine on a level surface, disengage the blade-control switch (PTO), and engage the parking brake.2.  **Shut down and wait:** Shut off the engine, remove the key, and wait for all moving parts to stop before leaving the operating position.3.  **Set the cutting height:** Lower the mower to the 76 mm (3 inches) height of cut.4.  **Remove the belt covers.** <PIC>5.  **Release the tension:** Use a 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole in the idler arm to remove tension on the idler spring. <PIC>6.  **Remove the old belt:** Remove the belt from the mower-deck pulleys and the clutch pulley.7.  **Remove the belt guide:** Remove the belt guide on the spring-loaded idler arm.8.  **Take out the existing belt.**9.  **Install the new belt:** Install the new belt around the mower pulleys and the clutch pulley under the engine. <PIC>10. **Reinstall the guide:** Install the belt guide on the idler arm.11. **Re-tension the spring:** Using the 3/8-inch ratchet in the square hole, install the idler spring. Ensure that the spring ends are seated in the anchor grooves.12. **Reinstall the belt covers.** <PIC>After installation, confirm that the belt is correctly seated around all pulleys and that the belt covers are securely fastened.", ["Manual23_99", "Manual23_100", "Manual23_100", "Manual23_101"]

"To set up the control of your over-the-range microwave, you can customize its default settings, including sound, clock, display speed, and defrost weight units. Here’s how:1.  **Touch the "Control Set-Up" pad.** This pad is used to change the oven's default settings.2.  **Choose your setting.** You can adjust the following options:    *   Sound (on/off)    *   Clock (12-hour or 24-hour format)    *   Display speed (slow, normal, or fast)    *   Defrost weight (Lbs or Kg)3.  **Follow the prompts in the Display.** The display will guide you through the available options for each setting. Use the number pads to make your selections as prompted.For details on the control panel layout, see the image below.<PIC>", ["Manual24_13"]

"To set the Light Timer on your over-the-range microwave, follow these steps. This will turn the cooktop light on and off automatically at the same times every day.**Example: To turn the light on at 2:00 AM and off at 7:00 AM.**1.  Touch the **Light Timer** pad.      <PIC>2.  Enter the time you want the light to turn **ON** (e.g., touch **2, 0, 0** for 2:00).3.  Touch the **Light Timer** pad again.4.  Enter the time you want the light to turn **OFF** (e.g., touch **7, 0, 0** for 7:00).5.  Touch the **Light Timer** pad once more to confirm and start the timer.**To cancel the Light Timer:**If you want to cancel the Light Timer while it is in operation, simply touch the **Light HI/LO/Off** pad.  <PIC>**Note:** To reset the on and off times, repeat steps 1 through 5.", ["Manual24_11", "Manual24_12"]

"You can set a favorite recipe by first programming the cooking instructions you want to save, then recalling it later with a single touch.Here’s how to do it:**To Save a Favorite Recipe:**1.  Program the cooking instructions you want to store. For example, to program cooking for 2 minutes.    <PIC>2.  Before touching Start, touch and hold the **Favorite Recipe** button. This saves the cooking cycle into memory.**To Recall and Use a Favorite Recipe:**1.  Simply touch the **Favorite Recipe** button.    <PIC>2.  The saved cooking instructions will begin immediately.**Additional Notes:**-   When the cook time is over, you will hear four beeps and "END" will display.-   The power level will default to Hi, but this setting is modifiable when you are programming the recipe.", ["Manual24_16", "Manual24_17"]

"You can reheat food using the **Reheat (Sensor)** function, which automatically sets the time and power level for you based on the food category.Here’s how to use it:1.  **Select the Food Category:** The Reheat function has preset power levels for 3 categories: **Casserole, Dinner Plate, and Soup/Sauce**.    <PIC>2.  **Start the Cycle:** As shown in the example for reheating a Casserole, simply press the corresponding button or follow the display prompts to start the program.    <PIC>3.  **Complete:** When the cook time is over, you will hear four beeps and **END** will display.**Recommended Amounts:**<PIC>**Note:** You can also use the **Hold Warm** function to keep food warm for up to 99 minutes and 59 seconds after reheating. While programming the Reheat instructions, just touch **Hold Warm** before touching **Start**. When the cooking cycle is over, you will hear two tones and “WARM” will appear in the oven display.", ["Manual24_29", "Manual24_30", "Manual24_31"]

"To use Auto Defrost, touch the **Auto Defrost** pad to select the food type (meat, poultry, or fish) and then enter the net weight of the food in pounds and tenths of pounds. The microwave is preset with three defrost sequences, and using this function is the best way to defrost frozen foods. <PIC>For example, to defrost 1.2 lbs of ground beef:1.  Touch the **Auto Defrost** pad.2.  Select the food type.3.  Enter the weight (1.2).4.  Touch the **START** pad. The display will change to a defrost time countdown. <PIC>During the cycle, the oven will beep. At this time, open the door and turn, separate, or rearrange the food. Remove any portions that have thawed, then return the frozen portions to the oven and touch **START** to resume the defrost cycle.For best results, follow these guidelines:- Enter the net weight only (the weight of the food minus the container).- Auto Defrost works best for raw food taken directly from a freezer that is 0°F or below.- If the food has been stored outside the freezer for up to 20 minutes, enter a lower food weight to prevent cooking the edges.- Separate pieces as they begin to defrost to allow for more even thawing.", ["Manual24_32", "Manual24_33"]

"To clean the grease filter:1.  Remove the filter by sliding it to the side, pulling it downward, and pushing it to the other side. The filter will drop out.    <PIC>2.  Wash the filter in the dishwasher or soak it in hot water with a mild detergent. Rinse well and shake to dry. Do not use ammonia, and note that the aluminum filter will darken.    <PIC>3.  Reinstall the filter by sliding it into the side slot, then pushing it up and toward the oven center to lock it in place.    <PIC>The grease filter should be cleaned often, at least once a month.", ["Manual24_47", "Manual24_48", "Manual24_49"]

"To replace the charcoal filter, follow these steps:1.  **Disconnect power:** Unplug the oven or turn off the power at the main power supply.2.  **Remove the vent grill:** Remove the two vent grill mounting screws (the 2 middle screws).    <PIC>3.  Take off the grill by tipping it forward, then lifting it out.4.  **Remove the old filter:** Take out the old charcoal filter.    <PIC>5.  **Install the new filter:** Slide a new charcoal filter into place. The filter should rest at the angle shown.    <PIC>6.  **Reassemble:** Slide the bottom of the grill into place. Push the top until it snaps into place. Replace the mounting screws. Turn the power back on at the main power supply and set the clock.", ["Manual24_50", "Manual24_51", "Manual24_52"]

"Here's how to replace the oven light:1.  **Disconnect power:** Unplug the oven or turn off the power at the main power supply.2.  **Remove the vent cover:** Take out the two middle mounting screws that secure the vent cover.3.  **Tip and lift the cover:** Tip the cover forward, then lift it out to remove it.    <PIC>4.  **Remove the bulb holder:** Take out the bulb holder.    <PIC>5.  **Lift the bulb holder.**    <PIC>6.  **Replace the bulb:** Replace the old bulb with a new 30 or 40 watt appliance bulb.7.  **Reinstall the bulb holder:** Put the bulb holder back into place.8.  **Reattach the vent cover:** Slide the bottom of the vent cover into place. Push the top until it snaps into place, then replace the mounting screws.9.  **Restore power:** Turn the power back on at the main power supply or plug in the oven.Confirm that the vent cover is securely snapped in and the screws are tightened before restoring power.", ["Manual24_54", "Manual24_55", "Manual24_56"]

"To set the PCI Express 3.0 x16 slots, you need to adjust the link speed in the BIOS. The available options are [Auto], [Gen1], [Gen2], and [Gen3].1.  Enter the BIOS setup during system startup.2.  Navigate to the **PCIEx16_1/ PCIEx16_2 Link Speed** setting.3.  Select the desired speed for slot 1 or slot 2.     <PIC>4.  Alternatively, look for the **SA - PCI Express options** menu and configure the **PCIe Speed** setting. The options are the same: [Auto], [Gen1], [Gen2], [Gen3].**Important notes for installation:**-   For a single graphics card, use the gray PCIe 3.0 x16_1 slot for better performance.-   Ensure you provide sufficient power when running certain modes.-   When using multiple graphics cards, connect a chassis fan to the CHA_FAN1/2/3 connector for a better thermal environment.", ["Manual25_31"]

To set the jumpers on a motherboard, you adjust the small plastic-covered pins on the board to configure specific hardware settings. The exact method depends on the jumper type. Here are the general steps:1.  **Locate the correct jumper**: Refer to the **Chapter 1: Product introduction** section of your motherboard manual, which includes "descriptions of the switches, jumpers, and connectors on the motherboard" to find the exact location and function of the jumper you need to change.2.  **Turn off the power**: Completely shut down your computer and unplug the power cord from the electrical outlet before touching any internal components.3.  **Move the jumper cap**: A jumper cap typically covers two of three pins.    *   To **short** or enable a setting, place the cap over the designated pair of pins.    *   To **open** or disable a setting, remove the cap entirely or place it over a single pin for safekeeping, depending on the manual's instructions.4.  **Verify the configuration**: Double-check that the jumper cap is sitting securely on the correct pins as shown in the manual's diagram.For a more detailed breakdown of all jumper configurations, you can consult **Chapter 1.6: Jumpers** in the manual.

"You can configure the onboard LEDs of your motherboard through two main methods: via the **BIOS** or through the **LED Control app in Ai Suite 3**.Here is how to adjust the different onboard LEDs:### 1. Standby Power LED (SB_PWR)This LED is a power indicator and its behavior **cannot be changed**. It lights up to show that the system is ON, in sleep mode, or in soft-off mode. It serves as a reminder to shut down the system and unplug the power cable before installing or removing any components. The image below shows its location.<PIC>### 2. Model Name LEDsThese are a group of five small LEDs located right below the printed model name. They light up in two modes to highlight the model name.<PIC>You can turn them off or change the lighting mode using these steps:*   **In BIOS:** Navigate to **Advanced** > **Onboard Devices Configuration** > **Model Name LED Lighting**. Here you can choose from the following options: **[Disabled]**, **[Still Mode]**, and **[Breathing Mode]**.*   **In Ai Suite 3:** Open the **LED Control** app to adjust the settings.<PIC>### 3. SupremeFX LEDThis LED outlines the audio components and lights up in three different ways to provide a lighting effect.<PIC>You can turn it off or change the lighting mode using these steps:*   **In BIOS:** Navigate to **Advanced** > **Onboard Devices Configuration** > **SupremeFX LED Lighting**.*   **In Ai Suite 3:** Open the **LED Control** app to adjust the settings.<PIC>", ["Manual25_54", "Manual25_55", "Manual25_56", "Manual25_57", "Manual25_58"]

To update the BIOS file of a motherboard, you need to prepare the necessary items and follow the correct procedure. Here are the key preparation steps and methods:### Preparation Before Updating1.  Prepare the motherboard support DVD and a USB flash drive.2.  Download the latest BIOS file and BIOS Updater, then save them to your USB flash drive.    *   **Note:** NTFS is not supported under the FreeDOS environment. Ensure your USB flash drive is in single partition and in FAT32/16 format.3.  Turn off the computer.4.  Ensure that your computer has a DVD optical drive.### Recovery Method: CrashFree BIOS 3If the BIOS fails or gets corrupted during the update, you can use the **CrashFree BIOS 3** utility to restore it. You can restore a corrupted BIOS file using the motherboard support DVD or a USB flash drive containing the updated BIOS file.*   Before using this utility, rename the BIOS file on the removable device to **Z17PG.CAP**.*   The BIOS file on the support DVD may not be the latest version. It is recommended to download the latest BIOS file.

"You can create a RAID configuration on this motherboard through the onboard Intel® chipset by connecting your drives to the Intel® Serial ATA 6.0Gb/s connectors and enabling RAID mode in the BIOS.Here are the main steps:1.  **Connect your hard disk drives** to the Intel® SATA 6.0Gb/s connectors (labeled SATA6G_1~6, SATA EXPRESS) on the motherboard using Serial ATA 6.0Gb/s signal cables.    <PIC>    <PIC>2.  **Change the SATA mode in the BIOS from AHCI to RAID.** The connectors are set to [AHCI] by default. You have two ways to do this:    *   **Using the EZ Tuning Wizard:** Press F11 during boot to access the EZ Tuning Wizard, which allows you to switch the motherboard's SATA mode from AHCI to RAID.    *   **Manually in the BIOS:** Go to the **SATA Configuration** section (refer to section 2.6.5 in the manual) and set the "SATA Mode" item to [RAID].3.  **Enable the specific RAID level.** In the BIOS, find the **RAID 0 / RAID 1 / RAID 10 / RAID 5** option and set it to **[Enabled]**.4.  **Create the RAID set.** Before creating the RAID set, refer to the manual bundled on the motherboard support DVD for detailed instructions on configuring the RAID array (RAID 0, 1, 5, or 10) through the Intel® chipset.", ["Manual25_48", "Manual25_49"]

"You can use **Method 1: Using SATA ODD & USB devices** to load USB 3.0 drivers and install the OS using a USB device. This method requires a support DVD, an OS 7 installation source, a SATA ODD, and a USB device (ODD or storage with 8 GB or more capacity).Here are the steps:1.  **Prepare the USB device:** Insert the OS 7 installation DVD into a USB ODD, or copy all files from the OS 7 installation DVD to a USB storage device on a working system.2.  **Connect the devices:** Connect the USB ODD or USB storage device to your 100 series platform, and insert the support DVD into a SATA ODD on the same platform.3.  **Boot and select the device:** Power on your system and press **F8** during POST (Power-On Self Test) to enter the boot screen.    <PIC>4.  **Automatic driver loading:** The USB 3.0 driver will be loaded automatically during installation startup.    <PIC>    The "Setup is starting..." screen will show up if the USB 3.0 driver is loaded correctly.5.  **Complete the installation:** Follow the onscreen instructions to complete the OS 7 installation.", ["Manual25_87", "Manual25_88"]

"To secure the motherboard to the chassis, place nine screws into the holes indicated by circles.  <PIC>  Make sure to place this side towards the rear of the chassis. Do not over tighten the screws, as doing so can damage the motherboard.", ["Manual25_6"]

"To prepare for installing the CPU, follow these steps:1.  **Unplug the power cord** from the wall socket before touching any component.2.  **Ground yourself** by using a grounded wrist strap or touching a safely grounded metal object (like the power supply case) to avoid static electricity damage.3.  **Handle components by the edges** to avoid touching the ICs on them.4.  Ensure the **ATX power supply is switched off** or the power cord is detached. Failure to do so may cause severe damage to the motherboard or components.    <PIC>5.  Verify you have the correct CPU designed for the **LGA1151 socket only**. Do not install a CPU designed for LGA1150, LGA1155, or LGA1156 sockets.    <PIC>6.  Check the motherboard socket: ensure the **PnP cap is on the socket** and the socket contacts are not bent. Contact your retailer immediately if the cap is missing or if you see any damage.7.  Ensure that the CPU is firmly clicked into place **before** installing it onto the CPU socket on the motherboard. Use the CPU Installation Tool for installing the CPU only; do not damage or bend the tool.    <PIC>    <PIC>    <PIC>8.  If necessary, apply the Thermal Interface Material to the CPU heatsink and CPU before you install the heatsink and fan assembly.    <PIC>    <PIC>**Important reminders:***   The product warranty does not cover damage to socket contacts resulting from incorrect CPU installation/removal, or misplacement/loss/incorrect removal of the PnP cap.*   Keep the PnP cap after installing the motherboard, as it is required for Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) requests.*   Always firmly hold both sides of the CPU Installation Tool when installing, removing, or picking it up.", ["Manual25_5", "Manual25_9", "Manual25_10", "Manual25_11", "Manual25_12", "Manual25_13", "Manual25_14"]

I can provide a general explanation. A Serial port connector (often a 10-1 pin COM header) on a motherboard is an internal connector that provides a legacy RS-232 serial communication port. The '10-1 pin' designation typically refers to a 2x5 pin header block with one pin missing (keyed) for correct orientation. It allows you to connect a serial port bracket to the back of a computer case for use with older peripherals, industrial equipment, or debugging tools. If you need specific pinout details or troubleshooting for your particular motherboard model, please refer to its manual.

"The TPM connector (14-1 pin TPM) on the motherboard is designed to support a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) system. This system securely stores keys, digital certificates, passwords, and data. It also helps enhance network security, protect digital identities, and ensure platform integrity. <PIC>", ["Manual25_40"]

"The Thermal sensor connector (2-pin T_SENSOR) is for the thermistor cable that allows you to monitor the temperature of your motherboard’s critical components and connected devices.  <PIC>", ["Manual25_43"]

"Based on the provided manual content, the specific steps for performing a "quick release" are not detailed. However, the manual provides crucial safety information and context for releasing pressure:You must ensure the appliance has de-pressurized completely before attempting to open it. A key indicator is the float valve. The unit is de-pressurized when the float valve has descended, and its top is flush with the lid.<PIC>**Crucial Safety Warning:** When pressure cooking, do not open the appliance until it has de-pressurized and all internal pressure has been released. If the float valve is still up and/or the lid is difficult to open, this indicates that the appliance is still pressurized—do not force it open. Any pressure in the appliance can be hazardous. Opening the appliance while it is still pressurized may lead to a sudden release of hot contents and may cause burns or other injuries.The manual references "Releasing pressure: Venting methods" for complete information on how to release pressure, but the specific step-by-step instructions for a quick release method are not included in the provided text.", ["Manual30_18"]

"To use the pressure cooking lid, you'll need to know how to install the float valve, close the lid, and remove it. Here's how:1.  **Install the float valve:** Drop the narrow end of the float valve into the hole on the top of the lid. Place one finger on the flat top of the float valve, then turn the lid over. Firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve. Do not attempt to operate the pressure cooker without the float valve and/or silicone cap properly installed.    <PIC>2.  **Close the lid:** Align the symbol on the lid with the symbol on the cooker base, then lower the lid onto the track. Turn the lid clockwise until the symbol on the lid aligns with the symbol on the cooker base.    <PIC>3.  **Remove the lid (after releasing pressure):** Grip the lid handle and turn it counterclockwise to align the symbol on the lid with the symbol on the rim of the cooker base. Lift the lid up and off the cooker base towards your body.    <PIC>", ["Manual30_37", "Manual30_13", "Manual30_12"]

"The float valve must be installed before use and does not require manual setting during cooking. It works automatically with the silicone cap to seal in pressurized steam.  To ensure it is ready:  1. Make sure the float valve is properly in place. It should descend and sit flush with the lid when the appliance is de‑pressurized.     <PIC>  2. Confirm the silicone cap is also fitted correctly, as both parts work together.  Do not attempt to operate the appliance without the float valve installed, and do not touch it during use.", ["Manual30_18"]

"To install the steam release valve, place it on the steam release pipe and press down firmly. It will sit loosely on the pipe when properly installed but should remain in place when the lid is turned over.  <PIC>  To remove it, simply pull the steam release valve up and off the steam release pipe.  <PIC>  **Safety Warning:** The steam release valve is integral to product safety and necessary for pressure cooking. Steam ejected from the valve is hot. Do not cover or block the steam release valve in any way, and do not place hands, face, or any exposed skin over it when releasing pressure.  <PIC>", ["Manual30_33", "Manual30_32", "Manual30_15"]

"To install the anti-block shield, place it over the prongs and press down until it snaps into position.  <PIC>To remove it, grip the lid like a steering wheel and use your thumbs to press firmly against the side of the anti-block shield (pressing towards the side of the lid and up) until it pops off the prongs underneath.  <PIC>Do not attempt to operate the pressure cooker without the anti-block shield installed, as it prevents food particles from coming up through the steam release pipe and is necessary for pressure cooking.  <PIC>", ["Manual30_35", "Manual30_34", "Manual30_19"]

"To install the float valve and silicone cap on your multi-use pressure cooker and air fryer, follow these steps:1.  **Insert the Float Valve:** Drop the narrow end of the float valve into the float valve hole on the top of the lid.    <PIC>2.  **Attach the Silicone Cap:** Place one finger on the flat top of the float valve, then turn the lid over. Firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve.**Important Safety Reminders:***   These parts must be installed before use. Do not attempt to operate the cooker without the float valve and silicone cap properly installed.*   The float valve and silicone cap work together to seal in pressurized steam. Do not touch the float valve during use.*   When the appliance has de-pressurized, the float valve will descend and its top will be flush with the lid.    <PIC>", ["Manual30_37", "Manual30_18"]

"To install the condensation collector, align the grooves on the collector with the tabs on the cooker base and slide it into place. Push it gently until it sits flush against the cooker base.  <PIC>  1. Locate the tabs on the cooker base and the corresponding grooves on the condensation collector.  2. Slide the collector straight onto the base, making sure the tabs engage with the grooves.  3. Push the collector in until it is fully seated and does not wobble.  Once installed, confirm the collector is secure and not loose.", ["Manual30_0"]

"To install the sealing ring:1.  Place the sealing ring over the sealing ring rack.2.  Press it into place, pressing down firmly to prevent puckering.    <PIC>3.  When installed properly, the sealing ring is snug behind the sealing ring rack and should not fall out when the lid is turned over.Tip: The sealing ring is easiest to install when it is wet and soapy.", ["Manual30_31"]

"To remove the silicone sealing ring, grip the edge of the silicone and pull the sealing ring out from behind the circular stainless-steel sealing ring rack. <PIC>For easier handling, it is best to remove the ring when it is wet and soapy.After removal, inspect the steel rack to make sure it is secured, centered, and at an even height all the way around the lid. Do not attempt to repair a deformed sealing ring rack.", ["Manual30_30"]

"The sealing ring creates an air‑tight seal between the pressure cooking lid and the inner pot when the lid is closed.  <PIC>  - **Installation** – It must be installed before cooking, and only one sealing ring should be fitted in the lid at a time.  - **Inspection** – Always check for cuts, deformation, and correct placement before cooking.  - **Replacement** – Replace the sealing ring every 12–18 months, or sooner if you notice stretching, deformation, or damage. Do not use a stretched or damaged ring.  - **Odour tip** – Silicone is porous and absorbs strong aromas/flavours. Keeping extra sealing rings on hand helps limit flavour transfer between dishes.  - **Safety** – Only use authorised Multi‑use pressure cooker and air fryer sealing rings. Using an unsuitable ring may cause food to discharge, leading to personal injury or property damage.", ["Manual30_16"]

"Based on the provided manual, here's what you should know about the steam release valve, which is referred to as the float valve:The float valve and its silicone cap work together to seal in pressurized steam. These parts must be installed before use. Do not attempt to operate the appliance without the float valve properly installed.The float valve also acts as a visual indicator of the unit's pressure state. When the appliance has depressurized, the float valve descends and its top becomes flush with the lid. <PIC>For your safety, do not touch the float valve during use.", ["Manual30_18"]

"The two primary modes are **Halo Mode** and **Virtual Wall Mode**.  - **Halo Mode**: Creates a protective circular zone with an invisible barrier that reaches approximately 24 inches from the center of the device. This prevents the vacuum from bumping into items you want to protect (e.g., a dog bowl or vase) or crossing into undesired areas (e.g., a corner or under a desk).    <PIC>  - **Virtual Wall Mode**: Functions as a virtual wall to block openings of up to 10 feet. When your vacuum detects a problem, it will alert you with a two-tone distress sound followed by a spoken message, and the troubleshooting indicator will blink. Here are the steps you can take:1.  **Reboot the vacuum** – For some errors, this may resolve the problem. Press and hold the CLEAN button for 10 seconds until all indicators illuminate, then release. You will hear an audible tone signifying a successful reboot.2.  **Check the app** – More detailed support and videos are available through the app and online.3.  **Confirm your schedule** – If you use the vacuum's scheduling feature, open the app after rebooting to confirm that the schedule remains intact.

"Before you ride, always perform the pre-operation checks. The short time spent checking the condition of the machine will be rewarded with added safety and a more reliable snowmobile. Here are the essential checks and preparations:1.  **Read the Owner's Manual and all labels** before operating the vehicle.2.  **Check throttle, brake, and steering** for proper operation before starting the engine.3.  **Set the parking brake** before attempting to start the engine. Never run the vehicle with the parking brake applied.4.  **Wear proper clothing** for both warmth and to help protect you from injury. This includes an approved helmet, eye protection, and adequate clothing for snowmobiling.5.  **Check the drive select lever position** (Drive, Reverse, or Low) before moving. Ensure the vehicle is at a full stop and the throttle lever is fully released before changing this position.6.  **Make sure the fuel tank cap is closed securely** after refueling.Also, remember these critical safety rules: do not operate the engine without the drive belt or drive guard, and never operate this vehicle on public roads. If you need to stop the engine in an emergency, push the engine stop switch down.<PIC>", ["Manual34_7"]

"Here are the detailed steps to start the engine, depending on your starting model.### Manual Starting Model1.  Turn the main switch to the “ON” position.2.  Pull slowly on the recoil starter until it is engaged, then pull it briskly.    <PIC>3.  After the engine starts, put the starter lever (choke) in the “Half-open” position. Warm up the engine until it does not run roughly or begin to stall when the starter lever is returned to the “Close” position.### Electric Starting Model1.  Turn the main switch to the “START” position.    <PIC>2.  After the engine starts, put the starter lever (choke) in the “Half-open” position. Warm up the engine until it does not run roughly or begin to stall when the starter lever is returned to the “Close” position.**Note:** The starter lever (choke) is not required when the engine is warm. If the engine is cold, ensure the starter lever is in the “Close” position before starting. The lights will come on after the engine starts.", ["Manual34_116", "Manual34_117"]

Turn the adjuster in or out until proper throttle lever free play is achieved.    <PIC>3. "The proper way to use the steering system on a snowmobile begins with a pre-operation check:1.  **Check for excessive free play:**    *   Push the handlebar up and down and back and forth.    *   Turn the handlebar slightly to the right and left.2.  **If excessive free play is noticed, consult a dealer.** Do not operate the snowmobile until the issue is resolved.<PIC>Always check the steering for proper operation before starting the engine.", ["Manual34_109"]

"To turn a snowmobile correctly, use your body weight and balance — this technique is often called “body English.” Here are the steps:1.  **Slow down** as you approach the curve.2.  Begin to **turn the handlebars** in the desired direction.3.  Put your **weight on the running board** to the inside of the turn.4.  **Lean your upper body into the turn**.    <PIC>5.  Lean more as the turn gets sharper or is made at higher speeds.Practice this procedure at slow speeds many times in a large, flat area with no obstacles before attempting higher speeds or tighter curves. If the snowmobile begins to tip, lean more into the turn to regain balance. If necessary, gradually let off on the throttle and/or steer to the outside of the turn.", ["Manual34_127"]

When riding uphill on a snowmobile, you should practice on gentle slopes first before attempting more difficult climbs. Here are the proper techniques:1. **Approach and Ascent**: Accelerate before you start the climb, then reduce the throttle opening to prevent track slippage.2. **Body Position**: Keep your weight on the uphill side at all times. On straight uphill climbs, lean forward. On steeper inclines, stand on the running boards and lean forward over the handlebars.3. **Cresting the Hill**: Slow down as you reach the crest, and be prepared to react to obstacles, sharp drops, or other people or vehicles that may be on the other side.4. **If You Stall or Can't Continue**: Do not spin the track. Stop the engine, set the parking brake, then pull the rear of the snowmobile around to point it back down the hill. Do not get on the downhill side of the machine. Once pointed downhill, restart the engine, release the parking brake, and descend the hill.

"When riding downhill, keep speed to a minimum. Apply just enough throttle to keep the clutch engaged while descending the hill — this allows you to use engine compression to help slow the machine and prevents the snowmobile from rolling freely down the hill. Also apply the brake frequently, with light pressure.<PIC>", ["Manual34_128"]

"The correct technique for crossing a slope (sidehilling) involves positioning your body to maintain balance and knowing how to react if the snowmobile begins to slip or tip.Here are the key techniques:1.  **Position Your Weight Uphill**    As you travel across the slope, lean your body to position your weight towards the uphill side. A recommended riding position is to kneel with the knee of your downhill leg on the seat and the foot of your uphill leg on the running board. This stance makes it easier to shift your body weight as needed.    <PIC>2.  **Reacting to a Sideways Slip**    Snow and ice are slippery, so be prepared for the possibility that your snowmobile could begin to slip sideways on the slope. If this happens, steer in the direction of the slide if there are no obstacles in your path. As you regain proper balance, gradually steer again in the direction you wish to travel.3.  **Regaining Balance if Tipping**    If your snowmobile starts to tip, steer down the hill to regain balance.**Safety Reminder:** Side hills are not recommended for a beginner or novice snowmobile operator. Practice these maneuvers on gentle slopes first.", ["Manual34_129"]

"Based on the provided manual, here are the steps for inspecting the spark plug on your snowmobile:1.  **Remove the spark plug.** You should periodically remove and inspect the spark plug because heat and deposits will cause it to slowly break down and erode.2.  **Check the coloration.** Inspect the condition of the spark plug by checking the color on the white porcelain insulator around the center electrode. The ideal color for a machine ridden normally is a medium to light tan.    <PIC>3.  **Measure the electrode gap.** Before installing any spark plug, measure the electrode gap with a wire thickness gauge and adjust it to the specification.    <PIC>4.  **Clean and reinstall.** When installing the plug, always clean the gasket surface, wipe off any grime from the threads, and torque the spark plug properly to **28 Nm (2.8 m-kg, 20 ft-lb)**.Do not attempt to diagnose engine problems yourself if the spark plug shows a distinctly different color (like a very white center electrode). Instead, take the machine to your dealer.", ["Manual34_138", "Manual34_139"]

"Here are the common causes and conditions that can lead to poor reception:**1. Weak or Fringe Area Signals**If your receiver is located in a weak, fringe area of a TV signal, your picture may be marred by small dots. It might be necessary to install a special antenna to improve the picture.<PIC>**2. Antenna Issues***   The signal from the antenna is weak.*   For outdoor antennas, severely weathered equipment and cables can reduce signal quality. Inspect them before connecting.*   Changing the direction or position of the antenna may improve reception.**3. Signal Interference (Ghosting)**Ghosts are caused when the TV signal splits and follows two paths. One is the direct path, and the other is reflected off tall buildings, hills, or other objects.<PIC>**4. Ignition Interference**The picture may flutter, drift, or suffer from black spots or horizontal streaking. This is usually caused by interference from automobile ignition systems, neon lamps, electrical drills, and other electrical appliances.<PIC>**5. Source Material**An old, bad, or illegally recorded tape is being played, or the program wasn't captioned when it was produced, transmitted, or taped.**6. Broadcast Type**Stereo or SAP can only be received if the TV station transmits those signals. Mono sound is automatically received if the broadcast is only in Mono, even if STEREO or SAP has been selected.", ["television0_03", "television0_02", "television0_01"]
